---
title: "Validation Walkthrough"
---

This is the actual example with code to run. 
The expected output is in the comments. 
Run it all from the project directory.

::: {.callout-note}
## You'll need
- [`uv`](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/), which gives you `uvx` and `uv run`
- the data-dict CLI, pinned to the commit this was built against:
  ```bash
  cargo install --git https://github.com/tidyverse/data-dict \
      --rev c146baec42fceb360252d7670663bddb1f6dcfc7 data-dict-cli
  ```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
Built against **data-dict CLI `0.0.1`**, commit
[`c146bae`](https://github.com/tidyverse/data-dict/commit/c146baec42fceb360252d7670663bddb1f6dcfc7)
(from `main`; no tagged releases yet). Generated files target spec
`$version: 0.1.0`. data-dict is still changing, so newer commits may behave
differently.
:::

## Part A: LinkML

Check the model, then check some data against it.

```bash
# lint the schema
uvx linkml lint otters.linkml.yaml
#   ✓ No problems found

# validate the schema against the LinkML metamodel
uvx linkml validate otters.linkml.yaml
#   No issues found

# validate a data instance against the schema
uvx linkml validate -s otters.linkml.yaml -C Survey survey.data.yaml
#   No issues found
```

The third command works on the nested form in `survey.data.yaml`. It's checking
enum values, numeric bounds, and that every otter's `territory: <n>` resolves to
a real `Territory`.

## Part B: generate the dictionary

```bash
# portable version
uv run gen_data_dict.py otters.linkml.yaml > data-dict.yaml

# version with source: pointers, for the data checks
uv run gen_data_dict.py otters.linkml.yaml --emit-source > data-dict.test.yaml
```

`--emit-source` adds `source: {parquet: <table>.parquet}` to each table. That's a
path, not data, so generation still reads nothing.

The stderr log for this schema is short:

```
# LOSS: schema prefixes/URIs dropped (data-dict has no URI concept)
# LOSS: class 'NamedThing': abstract/mixin flattened away (inheritance lost)
```

No unfilled columns. Every column got the representation its type requires,
straight from the schema.

## Part C: data-dict

```bash
data-dict validate-spec data-dict.yaml        # structure, no data needed
data-dict validate-meta data-dict.test.yaml   # column names and types vs Parquet
data-dict validate-data data-dict.test.yaml   # values vs constraints
```

Each one returns `ok`.

## Make sure it can fail

This example demonstrates data validation
failing. This can be seen by modifying `make_fixtures.py`
temporarily to associate an otter with a territory that 
doesn't exist — the `Territory` ids are `1`–`3`, so set
the otter `territory` column to something like
`[1, 2, 30]` in `make_fixtures.py` — then
re-check:

```bash
uv run make_fixtures.py
data-dict validate-data data-dict.test.yaml   # now D05 flags the orphan row
```

```
-- territory schema --
Schema({'area': Float64, 'protected': Boolean, 'id': Int64, 'name': String})

-- otter schema --
Schema({'species': String, 'weight': Float64, 'age_years': Int64, 'territory': Int64, 'id': Int64, 'name': String})
error[D05]: A foreign key's values must all appear in the primary key it references.
  --> data-dict.test.yaml:83:7
   |
43 | - name: Otter
...
79 |   - name: territory
...
83 |     - foreign_key
   |       ^^^^^^^^^^^ has 1 value not found in `Territory.id` (`30`; row: 3)
```

One caveat worth knowing: `validate-data` (data-dict 0.0.1) checks
required-not-null (D01), uniqueness (D02), enum membership (D04), and
foreign-key integrity (D05/D06), but 
as of July 22, 2026 it does *not* compare numeric values
against a column's `range`. The `range: [0, 45]` on `weight` is metadata that
`validate-spec` requires the column to *carry*; the Parquet values are never
checked against it. 

## What the run shows

The two toolchains express the same facts, just in different shapes:

| Fact | LinkML (nested) | data-dict (flat) |
|------|-----------------|-------------------|
| Species is one of three values | enum in `survey.data.yaml` | `values` on `species` |
| Weight is between 0 and 45 | `minimum_value` / `maximum_value` | `range: [0, 45]` |
| An otter belongs to a territory | `territory: 1` reference | `foreign_key` plus a join |
